active.title: Windows host color transport
active.summary: Queries Windows host foreground, background, and appearance state, frames replies without leaking control-string tails, and transports them as semantic client events under protocol 18.
reference_notes: Issue #1530 records the native Windows symptom. Issue #675, discussion #577, and issue #549 provide related host-theme and reply-framing context whose already-upstream portions are not re-carried.
active.remove_when: Upstream queries OSC 10/11 on Windows, transports bounded default-color and appearance events through both Windows input backends, and uses a protocol version newer than released protocol 17 for those wire variants.

active.title: Software cursor contrast
active.summary: Chooses a black or white software-cursor block from known cell backgrounds while retaining reverse-video fallback for unresolved colors.
reference_notes: No dedicated upstream issue currently tracks software-cursor contrast; issue #1530 is contextual.
active.remove_when: Upstream software-cursor rendering chooses a contrasting block for known named or RGB cell backgrounds and retains reverse-video fallback for unresolved colors.

active.title: Preserve host cursor color
active.summary: Queries the outer terminal's OSC 12 color, uses it for software cursors, and restores the exact host cursor color after redraw, normal exit, or panic.
reference_notes: No dedicated upstream issue currently tracks preservation of the outer cursor color; issue #1530 is contextual.
active.remove_when: Upstream queries OSC 12 on every foreground client, uses the result for drawn cursors, and restores the exact queried outer-terminal cursor color on normal exit and panic.

active.title: Child OSC 12 cursor colors
active.summary: Tracks child OSC 12 sets and OSC 112 resets through pane state and semantic rendering so native and software cursors reflect child-selected colors safely.
reference_notes: Issue #1234 is the exact remaining behavior. Issue #806 covers the already-upstream query-response behavior; issue #1530 is host-appearance context.
active.remove_when: Upstream tracks child OSC 12/112 state through pane, semantic-frame, and terminal-ANSI rendering, preserves encoder memory across full redraws, and independently restores transient cursor overrides.

active.title: Default theme follows host appearance
active.summary: Distinguishes an omitted auto-switch setting from explicit false so otherwise unconfigured themes follow the terminal's light or dark appearance.
reference_notes: Discussion #1532 describes the exact default-theme behavior. Issue #675 and discussion #577 are the earlier proposal context; issue #1530 records the Windows symptom.
active.remove_when: Upstream distinguishes omitted theme.auto_switch from explicit false, follows host appearance only for otherwise unconfigured themes, and documents the automatic catppuccin/one-light default in every maintained locale and config reference.

active.title: Windows system notifications
active.summary: Replaces the Windows notification stub with Unicode-safe Shell_NotifyIconW informational balloons while keeping sound policy separate.
reference_notes: Issue #1330 reports the missing Windows toast, but the source commits did not contain an upstream issue trailer.
verification: Manually trigger a background agent notification and observe one native Windows toast without an extra shell sound.
active.remove_when: Upstream Windows notification delivery displays Unicode-safe native system notifications and keeps duplicate shell audio disabled independently from Herdr sound playback.

active.title: Windows MediaPlayer path binding
active.summary: Passes spaced and Unicode media paths through a process-scoped environment variable so PowerShell's inline command cannot lose the path argument.
reference_notes: Issue #1657 documents the exact unbound -Command argument root cause; issues #1062 and #1330 are earlier Windows playback reports.
verification: GitHub Actions run 29894851735 validated the focused fix on Windows.
active.remove_when: Upstream Windows MediaPlayer playback binds literal spaced and Unicode paths without relying on positional arguments following powershell.exe -Command.

active.title: Controlled app-local ConPTY
active.summary: Loads a complete app-local ConPTY bundle only from the executable directory, never probes PATH, and otherwise falls back to system ConPTY.
reference_notes: Issue #761's unsafe bare-DLL search is already fixed upstream; this patch adds an explicit complete-bundle opt-in without restoring PATH probing. Issue #1528 is contextual Windows ConPTY/input validation from the source work.
verification: GitHub Actions Windows app-local ConPTY smoke test must observe the bundled x64/OpenConsole.exe process.
GitHub Actions Windows system fallback smoke test must pass with no app-local bundle.
active.remove_when: Upstream portable-pty supports an absolute executable-local ConPTY DLL with complete architecture-specific OpenConsole validation, no PATH probing, and system fallback when no bundle is present.
